title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
After an exchange regarding cash and the Twer brothers' escape from Black Mountain, Officer Vane informs Andrew Ga Twer that he will stay overnight with the state police. As the group returns to the mainland, District Attorney Escham expresses skepticism regarding Stephen's explanation that the Twer brothers fled their homeland and changed their names simply because of a child's motive. Ellery suggests the brothers were actually fleeing a more direct danger and advises a transatlantic investigation, specifically suggesting a telegram be sent to Yugoslavia.

While walking around the east side of the house with Professor Yaredli and Escham, the group is approached by Dr. Temple and Jonah Lincoln. Jonah, agitated by the strange events at Bredehede, expresses frustration over his sister Hester's recent return from Oyster Island. A sudden, piercing cackle from the house leads Dr. Temple and the others to the second floor, where they find Hester in a state of hysterical madness. Dr. Temple and Officer Vane attempt to calm her, but Hester directs intense, hateful verbal attacks toward Mrs. Bradley before collapsing.

After Dr. Temple clears the room to administer a sedative, Jonah explains that Hester had been infatuated with a man named Roman on Oyster Island but has decided to leave for New York to be alone. When Elijah asks why Hester is so opposed to Mrs. Bradley, Jonah's demeanor shifts, and he firmly dismisses the question, claiming he has no idea.
